action.reasoning: Observation: iter 16 OOM during model loading to GPU. Analysis: Loading full model on single GPU with device_map='cuda:0' caused OOM due to 3.8B params + caching allocator warmup. Need to split across GPUs or use 4-bit quantization. 4-bit would complicate merging and eval because predict.py must load 4-bit model, but eval container lacks bitsandbytes. Better to load full precision model with device_map='auto' and max_memory constraints, plus gradient checkpointing, to fit in 2×32GB GPUs. Also fix training loop: proper label masking, val IoU monitoring, and merging+save. Decision: Write new train.py with device_map='auto', max_memory={0:'25GiB',1:'25GiB'}, grad_checkpointing, batch_size=1, grad_accum=4, full dataset, 3 epochs, eval every 500 steps, save best adapter, then merge on CPU and save merged model. Copy base model to checkpoints/base_model for predict.py self-containment. Submit eval after training.
